QUEST_001_TRIGGER.text: Gather two rocks and a stick. Bring them to the crafting stone. You will make your first axe. Without it, you cannot hunt. Without hunting, you cannot survive.

QUEST_002_TRIGGER.text: You found the eastern ridge. Look at these tracks — deep, wide apart. The creature that made these is heavy. Heavier than anything I have seen. We do not follow it. Not yet. First, we need fire. Fire keeps them away at night. Without fire, we are just meat.

QUEST_005_UNLOCK.text: The pack hunts at dusk. Five of them — maybe six. They split apart, come from different sides. One makes noise to draw your attention. The others come from behind. I have seen it twice. Both times, someone died. If you hear one raptor, look for the others first.

dialogue_nodes.speaker: NPC_Elder
dialogue_nodes.text: Gather two rocks and a stick. Bring them to the crafting stone. You will make your first axe. Without it, you cannot hunt.

dialogue_nodes.speaker: NPC_Elder
dialogue_nodes.text: Near the water. Always near the water. The river smooths them. Look for flat ones — they hold an edge better.

dialogue_nodes.speaker: NPC_Elder
dialogue_nodes.text: Those tracks are from the big one. It has been here before — twice in the last moon. It does not come near fire. Build the campfire before dark.

dialogue_nodes.speaker: NPC_Elder
dialogue_nodes.text: Big enough to see from the ridge. Small fires go out. Small fires do not scare anything. Make it big.

dialogue_nodes.speaker: NPC_Elder
dialogue_nodes.text: The raptors will test us. They always do. They are smart — smarter than you think. But they are also afraid of what they do not understand. Use that.

dialogue_nodes.speaker: NPC_Elder
dialogue_nodes.text: Do not fight them in the open. Use the rocks, the trees. Make them come to you one at a time. A raptor alone is manageable. A raptor with its pack is death.

dialogue_nodes.speaker: NPC_Elder
dialogue_nodes.text: Then go. Do not waste daylight.
